description_long: This is a commemorative stone monument featuring a bust of André Le Nôtre, mounted on a tall pedestal and set within a partially enclosed stone alcove. The bust is rendered in dark stone or metal, depicting a detailed head and shoulders with curly hair. The alcove is constructed from large, light-colored stone blocks, with the name 'ANDRE LE NOTRE' and the years '1613-1700' inscribed prominently above the bust. Additional inscriptions on the back wall list Le Nôtre's achievements and locations associated with his work, such as Versailles and Chantilly. The base of the alcove is paved with stone, and the structure appears robust and monumental. The overall appearance is solemn and respectful, suitable for a public park or historical site.
description: A stone monument with a bust of André Le Nôtre set in an inscribed alcove, featuring detailed stonework and commemorative text.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bust of André Le Nôtre on a pedestal, framed by the stone alcove and inscriptions.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the back of the stone alcove with reversed inscriptions and no visible bust.
description_view_2: Side-rear view highlighting the thickness of the stone walls and partial visibility of the inscriptions.
description_view_3: Oblique front view showing the bust, pedestal, and the full extent of the inscribed alcove walls.
attribute_location_context: The monument is set in an outdoor, paved area, likely within a park or historical garden, as suggested by the stone paving and open surroundings.
